name: Trail at the summit of Montagnon
region_intro: Jogging routes around Bilhères are set within the Pyrénées-Atlantiques, offering diverse terrain from 650 to 750 meters altitude. The region features vast green pastures of the Plateau du Bénou, challenging mountain passes like Col de Marie-Blanque, and impressive mountainous surroundings. Five streams contribute to the lush landscape, providing varied running environments. Best jogging routes around Bilhères The most popular jogging route is The Bénou Plateau – Plateau du Bénou loop from Bilhères, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail that takes about 41 minutes to complete. This route leads through vast green pastures and offers panoramic views over the Ossau Valley. Another top favourite among local runners is Pic d'Escurets loop from Col de Marie-Blanque, a difficult 3.1 miles (5.0 km) path. This route features inclined terrain and rewarding vistas from the iconic Col de Marie-Blanque. Local runners also love the Small waterfall – Houn Barrade Pass loop from Bilhères, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail leading through lush landscapes with flowing water and mountain passes, often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Jogging around Bilhères is defined by vast green pastures, challenging mountain passes, and ancient historical sites. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate jogs to more challenging trail runs. The routes in Bilhères are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 25 reviews. More than 2,700 runners have used komoot to explore Bilhères's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Bilhères?
A: There are over 15 dedicated running routes around Bilhères, offering a variety of experiences from moderate jogs to challenging trail runs. The komoot community has explored these trails over 2,700 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Bilhères?
A: The terrain around Bilhères is incredibly varied, ranging from the vast green pastures and meadows of the &lt;em&gt;Plateau du Bénou&lt;/em&gt; to the inclined paths of challenging mountain passes like the &lt;em&gt;Col de Marie-Blanque&lt;/em&gt;. You'll find a mix of grassy paths, dirt tracks, and some sections with significant elevation changes, providing diverse running experiences.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Bilhères?
A: While many routes in Bilhères are rated as difficult due to the mountainous terrain, there are a couple of moderate options suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35411755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Bénou Plateau – Plateau du Bénou loop from Bilhères&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.1 km trail with relatively gentle elevation changes, perfect for enjoying the scenic pastures.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Bilhères area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Bilhères are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35411755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Bénou Plateau – Plateau du Bénou loop from Bilhères&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning panoramic views over the Ossau Valley. Another great loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36728973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small waterfall – Houn Barrade Pass loop from Bilhères&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through lush landscapes.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or attractions I might see while running in Bilhères?
A: The routes offer breathtaking scenery. You can explore the iconic &lt;strong&gt;Plateau du Bénou&lt;/strong&gt; with its vast pastures and ancient cromlechs (stone circles). The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marie-Blanque Pass&lt;/a&gt; provides rewarding vistas, and some trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36728973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small waterfall – Houn Barrade Pass loop from Bilhères&lt;/a&gt;, feature picturesque water features. You might also catch glimpses of peaks like Montagnon and MailhMassibé.

Q: Are there any running trails that feature waterfalls or significant water features?
A: Yes, Bilhères is crisscrossed by five streams, contributing to a lush landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36728973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small waterfall – Houn Barrade Pass loop from Bilhères&lt;/a&gt; is specifically named for its small waterfall and takes you alongside flowing water, offering a refreshing element to your run.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Bilhères?
A: The running routes in Bilhères are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 25 reviews. Runners often praise the stunning panoramic views, the varied terrain, and the peaceful environment that allows for an immersive outdoor experience.

Q: Is Bilhères suitable for family-friendly running or walking with children?
A: The &lt;em&gt;Plateau du Bénou&lt;/em&gt; offers several trails that are considered family-friendly, including routes that lead to historical sites like the ancient cromlechs. While many running routes are challenging, the more moderate options on the plateau can be adapted for family walks or gentle jogs, allowing everyone to enjoy the beautiful natural surroundings.

Q: Where can I park when accessing the running routes in Bilhères?
A: Parking is generally available at key access points to the trails, particularly around the &lt;em&gt;Plateau du Bénou&lt;/em&gt; and near the &lt;em&gt;Col de Marie-Blanque&lt;/em&gt;. Specific parking information can often be found on individual route pages on komoot, or by checking local signage upon arrival.

Q: What is the best season for jogging in Bilhères?
A: Bilhères offers great running opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer is also excellent, especially in the mornings or evenings, though some higher elevation routes can be challenging in direct sun. In winter, the &lt;em&gt;Bénou plateau&lt;/em&gt; transforms into a snow playground, suitable for snowshoeing, but dedicated running might be limited to lower, cleared paths.

Q: Are there any challenging trail running options for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. Bilhères is situated amidst impressive mountainous terrain, including the &lt;em&gt;Lazerque Massif&lt;/em&gt; and peaks like &lt;em&gt;Montagnon&lt;/em&gt;. Routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e878756180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail at the summit of Montagnon&lt;/a&gt; (over 15 km with significant elevation) or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26286441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic d'Escurets loop from Col de Marie-Blanque&lt;/a&gt; offer demanding climbs and rewarding views, perfect for experienced trail runners seeking a challenge.

Q: Can I find information about the natural and cultural heritage of the Bénou Plateau?
A: Yes, the &lt;em&gt;Plateau du Bénou&lt;/em&gt; is not only a natural wonder but also rich in cultural heritage. It's an emblematic pastoral area with traditional mountain huts called 'cuyalas' and ancient cromlechs (stone circles) dating back 3,000 years. For more detailed information about the plateau, you can visit the local tourism website: &lt;a href="https://www.tourisme64.com/en/activities/bilheres/PNAAQU064FS0003O-plateau-du-benou/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plateau du Bénou on tourisme64.com&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: The mountain of Bielle reaches 1973m of altitude. It is a superb observatory on the plain and on the high peaks of the Pyrenees chain (from the Pic du Midi de Bigorre to the Pic d'Orhy).
